problem_from_audit: Value proposition and CTA compete; stats clutter the first impression.
fix: Make one primary promise, one primary matcher/start path, and remove vanity stats from the hero.
acceptance_criteria: Within 5 seconds a first-time visitor knows the site helps choose a meal delivery service by meal type, country, diet, and budget.

problem_from_audit: Massive coverage table overwhelms homepage, best, review, comparison, and country pages.
fix: Render coverage table only on directory-style pages.
acceptance_criteria: No best/review/comparison/country page shows the giant brand table immediately below the hero.

problem_from_audit: Labels are too abstract for normal users.
fix: Use buyer mental-model labels: Top Picks, Compare Brands, Meal Types, Deals, Countries/Regions, All Brands.
acceptance_criteria: A non-expert can predict where to click for top services, brand comparisons, meal categories, regions, and the full directory.

problem_from_audit: Disclosure is too far from affiliate links.
fix: Add nearby disclosure text to sections containing affiliate links.
acceptance_criteria: Every section with affiliate CTAs has a nearby plain-language commission disclosure before or near the CTA.

problem_from_audit: Start path should guide by meal type, not database volume.
fix: Use six clear cards: prepared, meal kits, high protein, cheap, family, deals.
acceptance_criteria: The first decision point is meal type or situation, not a table or directory.

problem_from_audit: Top-picks pages need curated ranked lists before details.
fix: Add 3-6 ranked cards with best-for labels, pros/cons, pricing caveat, and Check price CTA.
acceptance_criteria: Each best page starts with a shortlist, not generic text or a table.

problem_from_audit: Reviews need structure and buyer analysis.
fix: Add overview, pros/cons, pricing/plans caveat, who it fits, alternatives, how we compare, verdict.
acceptance_criteria: A review page can stand alone as a buying decision page without the directory table.

problem_from_audit: Head-to-head pages need clearer verdicts and fewer distractions.
fix: Add winner summary by price, convenience, diet fit, country availability, and routine.
acceptance_criteria: A user can decide between two brands from the first comparison screen.

problem_from_audit: Country navigation is not clear enough for non-US users.
fix: Add country summary, local available categories, regional caveats, and local top brands.
acceptance_criteria: A user from the country can quickly see which services are relevant locally.

problem_from_audit: Trust signals are hard to find.
fix: Add last-updated, methodology link, source note, and why-you-can-trust-us module near recommendations.
acceptance_criteria: Every money page explains how recommendations are made and when they were updated.

problem_from_audit: Logo needs brand presence and padding.
fix: Use supplied orange mark, cropped for web, with internal padding and favicon references.
acceptance_criteria: Logo is visible at small sizes and not jammed against its container.

problem_from_audit: Pages feel too dark/busy in places.
fix: Move toward light comparison-guide surfaces, orange CTAs, less texture, more whitespace.
acceptance_criteria: The site feels like a premium guide, not a dark dashboard or database dump.

problem_from_audit: Directory still needs to be useful, not a junk drawer.
fix: Keep coverage table here, add search/filter explanation and warning that recommendations live on Top Picks pages.
acceptance_criteria: Directory is clearly a research tool, not the recommended first path.

fix: Add lightweight brand search/autocomplete once core pages are polished.
acceptance_criteria: Users can find a brand or country without scanning the directory table.

problem_from_audit: Dense pages must remain usable on mobile.
fix: Ensure header, matcher, cards, CTAs, and tables collapse cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: No horizontal overflow or clipped primary content on mobile.
